title: Chapter 24: The Vane Redemption
content: Isolde Vane came to Sera on a grey autumn morning, six months after the battle.

She was thinner than she'd been. Her auburn hair was pulled back in a simple braid — no elaborate arrangement, no silk ribbons. Her fine gown had been replaced by a practical wool dress. She looked like a woman who had spent half a year in exile on her family's diminished estate, which was exactly what she was.
"Your Majesty." Isolde curtsied — properly this time, without the knife's edge of mockery. "Thank you for seeing me."

"You asked for an audience." Sera's voice was neutral. She sat in the great hall, Kael's throne empty beside her — he was in the training yard with Darius, giving her space to handle this alone. "What do you want, Lady Vane?"
Isolde straightened. For a long moment, she just looked at Sera — really looked, the way she'd never bothered to look when Sera was just a wolf-less maid.
"I came to apologize," she said finally. "Not because my family told me to. Not because it's politically advantageous. Because I've had six months to think about what I did, and I... I was wrong."
Sera didn't respond. She let the silence stretch.

"I tried to have you executed," Isolde continued, her voice cracking. "I framed you for crimes you didn't commit. I spread rumors that turned the pack against you. I conspired with Aldric's people — not directly, I was too careful for that, but I knew what my steward was doing and I let it happen. I let rogues into the castle. I almost got the Alpha killed." She swallowed. "I almost got *you* killed."
"Why?" Sera's voice was quiet. "Why did you hate me so much?"

Isolde's composure cracked — just a hair, but enough. "Because you were nothing. You were a wolf-less maid. A servant. A nobody. And the Alpha — the Alpha I'd been raised to believe I would marry someday — looked at you the way he never looked at me." Her voice dropped. "I'd been told my whole life that I would be Wolf Queen. My family groomed me for it. Every lesson, every etiquette class, every political maneuver — it was all building toward that. And then you walked into the trial, and he commuted your sentence, and I knew. I *knew* I'd lost."
"But you kept fighting anyway."

"Because I didn't know how to stop." Isolde's eyes were wet. "I'm not making excuses. What I did was unforgivable. I just... wanted you to understand. It wasn't about you. It was never about you. It was about a lifetime of being told I was destined for something, and then watching it slip away to a woman who wasn't even trying to take it."
Sera studied her for a long moment. She could feel Kael through the bond — his steady presence, his trust in her judgment. *Whatever you decide, I'll support it.*
"You're right," Sera said finally. "What you did was unforgivable. Attempted murder. Conspiracy. Treason. Any one of those crimes carries a death sentence."
Isolde closed her eyes. "I know."
"But."
Isolde's eyes flew open.

"But," Sera continued, "the pack has changed. I've changed. The old way — eye for an eye, blood for blood — that's what kept us trapped for a thousand years. The Shadow fed on that darkness. On our fear. On our hate." She rose from her throne. "I'm not going to execute you, Lady Vane. I'm not going to exile you. I'm going to give you something harder."
"What?"

"A chance. To be better. To help instead of hurt. The pack's eastern territories need a governor — someone who understands politics, who can manage resources, who knows how to navigate noble houses without starting wars." Sera's eyes were steady. "It's a thankless job. It's far from the capital. You'll have no power except what you earn. And if you fail — if you scheme, if you conspire, if you hurt anyone ever again — I will personally ensure you never see the light of another moon."
Isolde stared at her. "You're offering me a *job*?"
"I'm offering you a chance to be the wolf you could have been. The wolf your family should have raised you to be." Sera descended from the dais. "Take it or leave it."
For a long moment, Isolde was silent. Then, slowly, she dropped to one knee.
"I'll take it," she said, her voice thick. "And I'll try. To be better. To be... worthy."
"That's all I ask."
Sera extended her hand. After a heartbeat of hesitation, Isolde took it.
"She took the job," Sera reported to Kael that evening. "She's leaving for the eastern territories at dawn."
"You're more merciful than I would have been." Kael's arms wrapped around her from behind. "I would have exiled her to the northern wastes."
"There aren't any northern wastes."
"I would have *made* wastes."
*"I STILL think we should have made the wastes. 'Isolde's Personal Wasteland.' It had POTENTIAL. But fine. Redemption arcs are good too. Character development. Growth. Yay."*
"Fenris is disappointed about the wastes," Sera said.
"Fenris can write a poem about it." Kael pressed a kiss to her hair. "You did the right thing. The pack doesn't need more blood. It needs wolves who can change."
"Everyone can change." Sera leaned back against him. "Some people just need a reason."
"You were my reason."
"And you were mine."

Outside the window, the autumn leaves were falling — gold and red and orange, swirling in the wind. The pack was healing. The wounds of the battle were fading. And somewhere in the eastern territories, a former enemy was beginning her own journey toward redemption.
The Moon Goddess, Sera thought, had an excellent sense of timing.
